title: NOC 32112 – Dental Technologists and Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Dental Technologists and Technicians under NOC 32112 are skilled laboratory professionals who design and fabricate dental prosthetics across Canada. With steady demand for restorative and cosmetic dental services, this TEER 2 occupation continues to support Canada’s expanding oral healthcare system. For qualified professionals with recognized credentials, NOC 32112 offers eligibility under Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs, providing a practical pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3211 – Technical occupations in dental health careNOC Code32112

duties: Dental Technologists and Technicians perform some or all of the following duties:
Design, fabricate, modify, and repair dental devices
Create crowns, bridges, inlays, onlays, clasps, and implants
Follow laboratory infection control and hazardous material procedures
Consult with dentists on complex cases
Conduct research in dental materials and techniques
Perform administrative duties within dental laboratories
employment_requirements: Completion of a college program in dental technology or
Four or more years of supervised on-the-job training
Registration with a provincial regulatory body required in all provinces except Manitoba and the territories
College of Dental Technologists of Ontario
College of Dental Technologists of Alberta
College of Dental Technicians of British Columbia
Ordre des techniciens et techniciennes dentaires du Québec
Internationally educated applicants must complete credential recognition and provincial registration before practice.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $31,800 to $67,400, depending on province, specialization, and whether self-employed.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 32112 is moderate nationwide. While national supply projections exceed demand, employment remains viable in:
Professionals with advanced digital dentistry skills (CAD/CAM systems) may experience stronger employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Dental Technologists and Technicians are employed in:
While supply may exceed demand nationally, regional opportunities remain available in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Quebec, and Saskatchewan.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32112?
Dental Technologists and Technicians benefit from:
Growing cosmetic and restorative dental services market
Access to universal healthcare and settlement benefits
Dental laboratory services remain a foundational component of modern dentistry in Canada.
pathways: If you are a qualified Dental Technologist or Technician, NOC 32112 may provide a pathway toward permanent residence in Canada.
Express Entry and PNP eligibility assessment
Credential recognition and provincial registration guidance
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your structured pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 32112.
